MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Aug-11-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Anne Brauer, Esq., of BUTY & CURLIANO (510-267-3000), appearing for defendant / moving party Truitt and White Lumber Co.
Oren P. Noah, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Levy Huges, Jr.

RULING:
DEFENDANT TRUITT AND WHITE LUMBER CO.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Truitt & White Lumber Co.'s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternative motion for summary judgment is denied as to negligence, strict liability and substantial factor causation and off calendar as to false representation, premises owner/contractor liability and punitive damages. As to negligence, strict liability and substantial factor causation, defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant, and that such exposure was a substantial factor. Plaintiff's responses to defendant's special interrogatories and his deposition testimony preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Moreover, defendant did not present any evidence that it propounded written contention discovery seeking the names of all persons with knowledge and all documents relating to plaintiff's allegation that asbestos attributable to defendant was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to plaintiff's asbestos-related illness. Plaintiff represents that he did not allege premises owner/contractor liability against defendant. Plaintiff dismissed his claim for false representation and request for punitive damages. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 (220/HEK)

RULING:
Defendant Elementis Chemicals' motion for summary judgment. Defendant submitted to the Court's tentative ruling and the ruling was adopted: Defendant's motion for summary judgment is denied.
